data.title: Emaar Properties to Halt Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Emaar Properties (DFM:EMAAR) will pause trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 9:30 am local time on Friday due to a board meeting.

The Dubai-based property developer will resume trading once the meeting results are disclosed, according to a same-day release.

data.title: Kyrgyzstan Industrial Production Rises in January
data.description:
Industrial production in Kyrgyzstan climbed 10.7% year-on-year in January 2025, compared to a 16.5% rise in the same month of the previous year.

Output rose for manufacturing (9.6%), mining (2.5%), supply of electricity, gas and air conditioner (22.3%), and water supply and waste management (11.5%).

data.title: Acroud has successfully completed the previously announced restructurin
data.description:
Acroud AB

ACROUD AB (publ) (“Acroud” or the “Company”) has successfully completed the restructuring that was announced on 9 December 2024.

All shares issued pursuant to Acroud's resolution on a new issue of shares resolved on at the extraordinary general meeting held on 24 January 2025, have been subscribed for, allotted to, and payed for in accordance with the notice convening the extraordinary general meeting. Upon registration of the new issue of shares with the Swedish Companies Registration Office, the following entities will thus hold the following ownership stakes in Acroud: RIAE Media Ltd, approximately 39 per cent; Strategic Investments A/S, approximately 9.5 per cent; SMD Group Ltd, approximately 9.3 per cent; PMG Group A/S, approximately 6.2 per cent; Nordic Sports Management ApS, approximately 2.1 per cent; and Double Down Media Ltd, approximately 0.8 per cent. In addition, holders of the Company's bonds (the "Bondholders") with ISIN SE0017562481 (the "Bonds") will hold an ownership stake of approximately 23.4 per cent. The shares will be allotted to the Bondholders pro rata on the basis of total holdings on the record date on 7 February 2025. The total nominal amount of the Bonds is SEK 80,812,386 following the set-off against shares in an aggregate amount of SEK 70,000,114.

The Company's super-senior bonds

Acroud has on 29 January 2025 issued super-senior bonds with ISIN SE0023615661. The total nominal amount of the super-senior bonds is SEK 65,312,500, which has been paid by a cash payment of SEK 26,125,000 and in an amount of SEK 39,187,500 by way of set-off against Bonds.

Delivery and the admittance for trading of the new shares

All new issued shares are expected to be registered with the Swedish Companies Registration Office on 17 February 2025. The shares are expected to be delivered and admitted to trading on Nasdaq First North Growth Market on 21 February 2025.

Changes in share capital and number of shares

Resolution was made at the Company's extraordinary general meeting on 24 January 2025 to reduce the quota value to EUR 0.0038631 per share in order to enable the subscription price of SEK 0.25 per share in the Company's new issue. As a result of this change and the new issue of shares, the share capital of Acroud will increase from EUR 4,366,824.859242 to EUR 4,624,171.801175. The total number of shares in Acroud will increase from 172,612,188 to 1,196,998,584.

Amended terms and conditions for the Bonds

The amended and restated terms and conditions for the Bonds have been entered into force and are available on Acroud's website, https://www.acroud.com/en/corporate-governance/bond-issue-2022/.

Advisors

Pareto Securities AB is acting as financial advisor to Acroud and Gernandt & Danielsson Advokatbyrå is acting as legal advisor to Acroud.

For further information, please contact:

Robert Andersson, President and CEO

+356 9999 8017

Andrzej Mieszkowicz, CFO

+356 9911 2090

ACROUD AB (publ)

Telephone: +356 2132 3750/1

E-mail: info@acroud.com

Website: www.acroud.com

Certified Adviser: FNCA Sweden AB, info@fnca.se

 

From May 2024 (Q1 Report) Acroud has changed reporting and company language to English. This means that Interim Reports and the correlated press releases will be issued in English only.

About ACROUD AB

ACROUD is a fast-growing global challenger that operates and develops comparison and news sites within Poker, Sports Betting and Casino. Acroud also offers SaaS solutions for the iGaming affiliate industry. In past years, a number of companies have joined the ride and thus several experienced individuals in the industry leads Acroud's journey to become "The Mediahouse of The Future". Our mission is to connect people, Content Creators (Youtubers, Streamers, Affiliates) and businesses. We are growing fast and remain a leading global player in the industry with just over 70 people in Malta, United Kingdom, Denmark and Sweden. Acroud has been listed on the Nasdaq First North Growth Market since June 2018.

EN Acroud - Press release on completed recapitalisation on 14 February 2025 (ENG)(6718620.3)

data.title: Aircraft maintenance boom has further to fly
data.description:
In 2024, stuttering production of new jets saw repairs of older models take on fresh import for the likes of Melrose and Safran. This year deliveries could rise by 25%. But supply chain uncertainty and strong flying demand mean the maintenance profit engine can continue to motor.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @Breakingviews on X

CONTEXT NEWS

France’s Safran reported full-year results on February 14. Adjusted revenue grew 17.8% year-on-year to 27.3 billion euros, while free cash flow rose from 2.9 billion euros to 3.2 billion euros.

Revenue in the company’s largest unit, Propulsion, whose activities include building and maintaining engines for civil and military aircraft, rose 15% on an organic basis to reach 13.7 billion euros, helped by civil aftermarket strength.

CEO Olivier Andriès said: “The operating margin grew by 150 basis points to 15.1% of sales, driven especially by strong aftermarket activity across the board, a relentless focus on operational excellence and the return to profitability of Aircraft Interiors”.

For 2025, the company raised previous outlooks for free cash flow and recurring operating income, helped by an increased forecast for growth in spare parts revenue.

GE Aerospace, Safran’s joint venture partner in engine maker CFM International, reported full-year results on January 23. Adjusted revenue rose 10% year-on-year to $35.1 billion. Its Commercial Engines and Services division grew 13%. The company forecast mid-teens growth in its CES unit in 2025.

Safran shares were up 0.5% by 0825 GMT on February 14.

data.title: German 10-Year Bund Yield Edges Up
data.description:
Germany’s 10-year Bund yield rose to 2.43% after US President Donald Trump signed a memorandum on reciprocal tariffs, though implementation was delayed to encourage negotiations.

Market sentiment also improved on hopes for a Russia-Ukraine peace deal, with Trump engaging Putin and Zelenskiy.

Meanwhile, money markets adjusted ECB rate expectations, pricing a December deposit rate of 1.94%, as investors await flash PMI to provide the first insights into how business conditions have fared in response to recent tariff announcements.

In the US, traders increased Fed rate cut bets to 33 basis points after inflation data.

In Germany, political uncertainty may persist after the February 23 federal election, as opposition leader Friedrich Merz and his CDU/CSU bloc will likely need to form a coalition to secure a majority.

data.title: Hoshizaki Logs 13% Boost in 2024 Profit
data.description:
Hoshizaki recorded a 13% rise in attributable profit for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024, to 37.2 billion yen from 32.8 billion yen in the year-ago period, a Thursday filing posted on the Tokyo bourse said.

Basic EPS was 258.49 yen in the year, up from 226.66 yen in the last fiscal year.

The industrial machinery company's net sales rose by 19% to 445.5 billion yen in the period from 373.6 billion yen a year prior.

The company declared a year-end dividend of 60 yen per share, bringing its total dividend for the fiscal year to 105 yen per share.

The company expects an attributable profit of 38.3 billion yen, a basic EPS of 270.84 yen, net sales of 460 billion yen, and a total dividend of 105 yen per share for the fiscal year ending Dec. 31.

data.title: HDFC Life Insurance to Raise Up To INR10 Billion Through Allotment of Debentures
data.description:
HDFC Life Insurance has received board nod to raise up to 10 billion Indian rupees through the allotment of non-convertible debentures on a private placement basis on Friday, according to a same-day filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The company will allot 100,000 senior, secured, rated, listed, redeemable, non-convertible debentures each having face value of 100,000 rupees.

The debentures will carry a coupon rate of 8.10% per annum and are scheduled to mature on Feb. 14, 2035.

data.title: Europe's Gas Price Drops Below 50-Euro Mark — Market Talk
data.description: European natural-gas prices extend losses in early trade, dropping below the 50-euros-a-megawatt-hour mark amid talks to end the war in Ukraine and discussions to ease EU storage targets. The benchmark Dutch TTF contract is down 4.1% to 49.30 euros a megawatt hour and on track to end the week 11% lower. A peace deal between Russia and Ukraine would ease fears of supply disruptions and potentially allow the resumption of Russian flows, further pushing down prices. Meanwhile, some EU countries expressed concerns about EU gas-refilling rules and rising prices, with Germany calling for exemptions from the targets this year, according to a Bloomberg report. Cold weather and low wind-power generation have pushed the EU to dip into its storage faster than expected in recent weeks, raising concerns over refilling requirements over the summer. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Market Chatter: HSBC Launches New Round of Job Cuts
data.description:
HSBC has started a new round of retrenchment at its investment banking business amid a restructuring of the lender, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

The latest job cuts will start in Asia and will cascade worldwide, according to the report.

The number of individuals who could be affected by the cuts is not clear yet, Bloomberg said.

HSBC is also gearing up for measures to save $1.5 billion in cost savings as a result of reforms by Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery, the Financial Times reported separately, citing two people familiar with the matter.

The bank declined to comment when reached by MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Nacon Partners with Evolution Championship Series for 2025 Gaming Tournament Series
data.description:
Nacon said Friday it partnered with the fighting game tournament Evolution Championship Series for the 2025 season.

The French gaming equipment maker will showcase its fight sticks, controllers, and headsets at Evo events, including Evo Japan, Combo Breaker, CEO, Evo Las Vegas, and Evo France.

The partnership includes branded commercial spots, live fan activations, and integration into Evo's broadcast segments.

data.title: Applied Materials falls after bleak Q2 revenue forecast
data.description:
** Shares of semiconductor equipment supplier Applied Materials drop 5% to $175.10 premarket

** Co forecasts Q2 revenue at $7.1 bln, plus or minus $400 mln, vs estimates of $7.21 bln - data compiled by LSEG

** U.S. restrictions on chipmaking tech exports to hurt 2025 revenue by about $400 mln, says CEO Gary Dickerson

** Sales to China accounted for about 31% of AMAT's total Q1 sales, down from about 45% a year ago

** Q1 results, however, beat estimates

** Peer Lam Research was off 1% in premarket trade

** Average rating of 36 analysts is a "buy", median PT at $210 - data compiled by LSEG

** AMAT stock near flat in 2024, up over 13% so far in 2025

data.title: Slovenia GDP Growth Slightly Eases in Q4
data.description:
Slovenia’s gross domestic product expanded by 1.5% year-on-year in the fourth quarter of 2024, slightly easing from an upwardly revised 1.6% growth in the previous quarter.

The economic slowdown was mainly influenced by softer increases in household consumption (1.2% vs 1.7% in Q3) and government spending (5.7% vs 9.2%).

Meanwhile, fixed investments declined at a softer pace (-5.2% vs -8.1%).

Additionally, net trade has added 1.3 percentage points to the GDP, as exports (3.9%) rose more than imports (2.3%).

On a seasonally adjusted quarterly basis, the economy advanced by 0.6%, following an upwardly revised 0.4% rise in the preceding period.

data.title: Oil refiners adding renewable feedstock to produce cleaner fuels, executives say
data.description:
Oil refiners are increasing their efforts to blend renewable feedstocks such as used cooking oil into their crude refining streams to meet global demand for cleaner fuels.

As a result, demand for catalysts and additives to remove the impurities from bio-feedstock has risen, Maurits van Tol, chief executive of catalyst technologies provider Johnson Matthey, said on the sidelines of the India Energy Week conference.

"What we see is alternative feedstocks, the blending in of some components with biomass background at refineries in the last couple of years," he said.

Demand for catalysts and additives is also getting a boost from the widespread usage of cheaper heavy crude which harms the units because of their higher sulphur content, van Tol said.

PETROCHEMICALS

Refineries are also pivoting towards making more petrochemicals to offset lower transportation fuels demand from the growth of electric vehicles.

"The configuration of new age refineries will have more lighter products like gasoline and naphtha," said Vartika Shukla, chairperson of state-run Engineers India.

In India, Bharat Petroleum targets increasing its petrochemical output to more than 40% of its portfolio compared with about 2%-3% at present through its new refinery in Southern India, said Sanjay Khanna, the company's director of refineries.

The company plans to build a new refinery with a capacity of at least 180,000 barrels per day in the state of Andhra Pradesh.

data.title: Malaysian Shares End Week Flat Amid Global Trade War Uncertainties; CelcomDigi Slides 9%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia ended the week almost flat amid global trade war uncertainties, even as the country's GDP expanded in 2024.

The benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI, the main gauge of Malaysian stocks, edged down by 0.68 points to end 0.04% lower at 1,591.60. The day range was between 1,587.58 and 1,595.01.

Malaysia's gross domestic product rose 5% year over year in the fourth quarter of 2024, according to a Friday press release by the Department of Statistics Malaysia. Growth in Q4 was slower compared with the 5.4% growth experienced in the third quarter, the department said.

Overall, the economy grew 5.1% year over year in 2024, in line with the flash estimate, but faster than the 3.6% expansion in 2023.

In other economic news, Malaysia logged a current account surplus of 11.4 billion ringgit in the fourth quarter of 2024, according to data from the Department of Statistics Malaysia released Friday.

In corporate news, CelcomDigi's profit attributable to owners fell to 157.0 million ringgit in the fourth quarter of 2024, from 435.2 million ringgit a year earlier. Its shares closed nearly 9% lower on Friday.

data.title: Gold Rises for 3rd Session
data.description:
Gold inched higher to almost $2,938 per ounce on Friday, reaming at record high level and heading toward a seventh consecutive weekly gain, on weaker dollar and as investors were analysing recent developments on US trade tariffs and interest rates.

On Thursday, President Donald Trump said that reciprocal trade tariffs would be imposed individually on countries and not be implemented immediately bringing the dollar to 2-month low.

On the other hand, the U.S. producer inflation data came in stronger than expected, following a hot consumer inflation report on Wednesday, reinforcing the view that the Federal Reserve would not be cutting interest rates in the near term.

data.title: Russian Rates Could Stay on Hold Following Ruble's Recovery — Market Talk
data.description: The Russian central bank is expected to hold interest rates at 21% in a decision at 1030 GMT as the ruble's recent recovery has likely helped tackle inflation, Commerzbank analyst Tatha Ghose says in a note. The currency's weakness after the last round of U.S. sanctions on Russia meant the central bank had to use large rate rises to counter the inflation pass-through, he says. The ruble has recovered substantially since then for "reasons not entirely clear," given USD/RUB and EUR/RUB are technical fixes that don't trade like normal floating exchange rates. However, cautious optimism over a Ukraine ceasefire deal could have offered some support. USD/RUB rises 0.4% to 89.8250 after hitting a five-month low of 89.2564 Thursday, according to FactSet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: China's Bank Loans Hit Record High in January
data.description:
Chinese bank loans hit a record high in January, official data showed Friday, as banks heeded Beijing's call to provide more support to the economy.

New yuan loans stood at 5.13 trillion yuan, equivalent to $703.72 billion, in January, up from 990 billion yuan in December, according to data released by the People's Bank of China.

The result beat the 4.3 trillion yuan anticipated by economists in a Wall Street Journal survey, and surpassed the prior record of 4.92 trillion yuan for January 2024.

Total social financing, a broader metric for credit that includes nonbank financing, stood at 7.06 trillion yuan in January.

China's M2, the broadest measure of money supply, rose 7.0% in January from the prior year, slower than December's 7.3% growth and the 7.4% expected by economists.

Write to Singapore Editors at singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Latour Buys US Heating, Cooling Systems Maker American Geothermal
data.description:
Investment AB Latour (LATO-B.ST) said Friday it purchased US-based heating and cooling systems maker American Geothermal to expand its footprint in North America.

The deal, conducted via the Swedish investment group's subsidiary Swegon, will increase the group's net debt to 12.7 billion kronor compared with December 2024.

data.title: DaVita down after Berkshire Hathaway cuts stake
data.description:
** Shares of DaVita falls 10.8% to $158 in premarket trade

** Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway sells 203,091 shares of the kidney dialysis services provider to bring its holdings down to about 35.89 million shares, a 45% stake worth nearly $6.4 billion

** Berkshire said sale was required pursuant to a share repurchase deal, under which DVA agreed on a quarterly basis to buy back enough shares to cut Berkshire's stake to 45%

** Berkshire has owned DVA shares since Q4 of 2011

** DVA gained 42.7% in 2024, and is up 18.4% YTD

data.title: Dollar drifts near multi-week low as tariff fears ease
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dollar hovers above near 3-week low as U.S. PPI report eases inflation concerns
  • Perceived room for tariff negotiations soothes trade war anxieties

The U.S. dollar stabilised around a near three-week trough on Friday as traders took solace that Washington's reciprocal tariffs were not immediately imposed, while a U.S. producer price report soothed inflation concerns.

U.S. President Donald Trump directed his economic team on Thursday to formulate plans for reciprocal tariffs on every country that imposes taxes on U.S. imports.

But he stopped short of quickly unveiling another round of tariffs, instead kicking off what could be weeks or months of investigation into other countries' levies imposed on U.S. goods.

That buoyed expectations that there may yet be room for target countries to negotiate, which helped shore up sentiment.

The dollar rallied 7% against a basket of currencies last year, as investors prepared for Trump's tariffs that threatened to fire up inflation, among other things.

Since taking office on January 20, Trump has announced tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China, but has these have been either delayed or watered down, which has dented the dollar.

The euro and the pound , which are in the firing line for reciprocal tariffs, have risen 2% and 3%, respectively, since Trump's inauguration.

"The lack of concern about the latest tariffs from Trump, suggests that the FX market continues to think that President Trump is posturing, and will tone down the tariffs at the last minute," XTB research director Kathleen Brooks said.

Some traders expect tariffs to benefit the dollar, but the delayed timeline of the newest announcements did little to lift the greenback off its weakest since late January following Thursday's wholesale inflation data.

The euro edged up to its highest in over two weeks against the dollar at $1.04823, supported by optimism around potential peace talks between Ukraine and Russia.

On Wednesday, Trump discussed the war in Ukraine in phone calls with Russian President Vladimir Putin and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy.

He said on Thursday that Ukraine would have a seat at the table during any peace negotiations with Russia.

Sterling , meanwhile, rose 0.18% on the day to $1.2591, its strongest since early January, after data earlier this week soothed some concern about the resilience of the UK economy.

SOFTENED INFLATION FEARS

Thursday's U.S. producer price index, which measures inflation at the farm and factory gate, showed some abatement in price pressures, particularly from a decline in energy costs.

That helped repair some of the blow to confidence from this week's hotter-than-expected consumer prices report, which prompted traders to rule out much more than one rate cut this year from the Federal Reserve.

Futures traders have about 33 basis points of cuts priced in for this year. That is up from 29 basis points before Thursday's data, but down from 37 basis points before the CPI data was released on Wednesday. (0#USDIRPR)

Uncertainty remains about the outlook for the U.S. economy, with questions about the way Trump administration policies will play out chief among them.

"We expect the Fed to remain cautious amid concerns about the stalled disinflation process and President Trump's tariff increases," said Carol Kong, a currency strategist at Commonwealth Bank of Australia.

U.S. retail sales figures for January are due later in the day.

U.S. Treasury yields declined as investors took comfort in the PPI numbers, helping the yen to claw back most of its losses after weakening to 154.80 on Wednesday.

The Japanese currency was steady at 152.775, having gained nearly 3% so far in 2025, as investors ramp up bets on the Bank of Japan raising rates again this year.

data.title: Emirates Central Cooling Systems Halts Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Emirates Central Cooling Systems (DFM:EMPOWER) shares temporarily paused trading on the Dubai bourse from 9:30 am local time on Friday due to its board meeting.

The cooling services company will resume trading after the publication of the meeting results, according to a same-day release.

data.title: China Credit Data Points to Continued Subdued Demand — Market Talk
data.description: China's latest credit and lending data suggests policymakers are still struggling to provide much of a lift to broad credit growth, Capital Economics says. China economist Zichun Huang attributes the January headline figures showing new local-currency loans at record highs to the usual seasonal pattern, noting that net lending is always strongest at the start of the year. To cut through the seasonality, CE looks at the data in terms of on-year growth in credit outstanding. On that basis, bank lending slowed from December to a fresh low last month, Huang writes. In the coming quarters, she thinks robust government bond issuance can lend support, but weak private demand will likely keep credit growth subdued. (fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com)

data.title: Indian shares fall as U.S. tariff worries persist
data.description:
India's benchmark indexes extended losses on Friday as investors fretted over the implications of Trump's plans to impose reciprocal tariffs, which analysts said could hurt the country the most among its Asian peers.

U.S. President Donald Trump is planning to slap reciprocal tariffs on every country taxing U.S. imports. The imposition of these duties, however, is likely to be delayed, helping global stocks stage a relief rally.

Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi met Trump on Thursday and offered to talk about easing tariffs, buying more U.S. oil and gas, combat aircraft and concessions.

But that did little to calm markets back home on worries that India could be hit hard by reciprocal tariffs. The Nifty 50 and the BSE Sensex lost about 0.8% each as of 2:25 p.m. IST.

"Indian markets have seen sharper losses on the day due to the high tariff differential with the U.S., compared to most other Asian economies," said Narendra Solanki, head of research at Anand Rathi.

Analysts said the potential consequences of U.S. tariffs on the Indian rupee and U.S. interest rates could trigger further foreign outflows, hurting domestic equities.

All 13 major sectors were down, with drug makers , which have significant revenue exposure to the U.S., falling the most with a 3.41% decline.

Telecom operator Vodafone Idea fell 5.6% and Bharti Airtel was down 0.1% after India's top court dismissed a set of review petitions filed to overturn the earlier order on the calculation of dues.

The smallcaps and midcaps tumbled 4% and 3%, respectively, and continued their downward trend on fears of slowing corporate earnings and stretched valuations.

The smallcap index is currently down about 21% from its record in December, while midcaps are 19.5% off from their peak closing level September.

data.title: Singapore Shares Edge 0.1% Lower; Banks End Mixed — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's FTSE Straits Times Index closed 0.1% lower at 3877.50, following two consecutive days of gains. Markets are likely digesting the changing U.S. tariff policies as well as the hotter-than-expected inflation data this week. Investors will be eyeing the U.S. retail sales numbers due later today for further clues on the health of the U.S. economy, the Commerzbank Research team wrote in a note. Among the biggest decliners, Singapore Exchange fell 5.8%, DFI Retail Group lost 2.6% and Singtel was 1.5% lower. Banks were mixed, with UOB and OCBC up 1.1% and 0.3%, respectively, while DBS Group shed 0.5%. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: Procore Technologies Is Maintained at Overweight by Piper Sandler
data.description: (09:33 GMT) Procore Technologies Price Target Raised to $110.00/Share From $90.00 by Piper Sandler

data.title: Emirates Reem Investments Halts Trading in Dubai
data.description:
Emirates Reem Investments (DFM:ERC) shares paused trading on the Dubai Financial Market from 9:30 am local time on Friday due to a board meeting.

The Dubai-based bottled mineral water company will resume trading after it publishes its meeting results, according to a same-day release.
